name: Bingin
notes: Mid tide is the safest quality window. Low tide is hollow and dangerous; high tide can still work but may have a stronger rip.
description: Bingin is a short, intense left-hand reef barrel on the Bukit Peninsula. The wave breaks close to shore over coral with a tight, competitive takeoff zone, then offers a predictable barrel and a short performance wall before the shallow Greedies end section. It is one of Bali's most consistent barrel waves but demands accurate positioning and strong reef experience.
hazards: heavy local and travelling surfer crowd
difficult entry and exit on lower tides
